article_title: US Clears Nvidia H200 Sales to Ten Chinese Firms With 25% Revenue Cut, But Beijing Tells Buyers to Stand Down
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent Analysis submission. Every quantitative claim and every direct quote verified verbatim across nine sources: 75,000-chip per-buyer cap (Reuters via BNN Bloomberg + Next Web), Trump 'chose not to / develop their own' Tom's Hardware quote verbatim, Lutnick 'blocked imports... steer investment toward domestic chipmakers like Huawei' verbatim, 'not a single Nvidia H200 chip has shipped' Tech Times framing verbatim, Trump 'a lot of different problems were settled' verbatim, Bessent 'some sort of emergency communication system' verbatim, 145%→30% / 125%→10% trade-truce tariff figures verbatim, Huang 'essentially zero / 95% market share' framing verbatim, $3.5-4B annual revenue restoration estimate verbatim, Huang 'benefits of spreading US technology' verbatim, Chris McGuire 'triple the amount of AI computing power' verbatim. Earnings detail: Tencent Q1 31.9B yuan capex / +63% / 165B yuan 2027 Goldman forecast verbatim (SCMP), Wu 'isn't a single card on our service that is idle' verbatim, T-Head 'scaled mass production' + 'compute scarcity' + 'structural advantage' verbatim, Mitchell 'dramatically increase capital spending / month by month' verbatim. December 8 + 25% Treasury framework verbatim (CFR), Intel-similar-opportunity language verbatim. Full Air Force One delegation list verified person-by-person against PBS NewsHour. Both internal cross-references (Zhipu GLM-5 Feb article and Xuantie C950 March article) verified to exist. The article also correctly hedges: 'pause is informal — a directive to Chinese buyers, not an explicit ban'.

source-0.status: Reuters via BNN Bloomberg. Verifies the 75,000-per-buyer cap, Alaska pickup of Huang, ten-buyer scope, Lenovo + Foxconn as distributors.

source-1.status: Tom's Hardware. Verifies Trump 'chose not to / develop their own' quote verbatim, Lutnick Huawei-substitution framing verbatim, third-party inspection + 25% Treasury fee structure, 95%→essentially-zero market-share characterization, $3.5-4B revenue restoration analyst estimate.

source-2.status: Tech Times. Verifies 'not a single Nvidia H200 chip has shipped' framing, Trump 'lot of different problems were settled' quote, 50% rare-earth shortfall, Bessent 'emergency communication system' quote verbatim, full 145%→30% / 125%→10% trade-truce tariff figures.

source-3.status: The National. Verifies Huang 'benefits of spreading US technology' framing verbatim, Chris McGuire 'triple the amount of AI computing power' quote verbatim.

source-4.status: PBS NewsHour. Verifies the full Air Force One delegation list — Musk, Cook, Fink, Schwarzman, Sikes, Fraser, Solomon, Culp, Mehrotra, Amon, McInerney, Thaysen, Miebach, Anderson, McCormick, Ortberg — name-by-name.

source-5.status: SCMP. Verifies Tencent Q1 31.9B yuan capex + 63% QoQ jump, Wu 'isn't a single card on our service that is idle' Alibaba quote verbatim, 165B yuan 2027 Goldman Sachs Tencent forecast.

source-6.status: The News International. Verifies Alibaba T-Head 'scaled mass production' + 'compute scarcity / structural advantage' quote verbatim, Mitchell 'dramatically increase capital spending / month by month' quotes verbatim.

source-7.status: CFR primary policy analysis. Verifies the December 8 announcement date, '25 percent of the revenue would go to the U.S. government as a tax' verbatim, the 'other tech companies like Intel would be offered a similar opportunity' framing.

source-8.status: The Next Web. Verifies the 75,000 per-license cap, 'Order books exist, but the deliveries do not' verbatim, China-share-shrunk-to-5%-from-pre-controls-above-20% framing verbatim, 'green light from Beijing' diplomatic mission framing.

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent Analysis-category piece. Multi-source corroboration of every fact. Honest hedging ('pause is informal — a directive to Chinese buyers, not an explicit ban'). Strong analytical layer in the political-economy section about the unusual 25%-Treasury-cut structure. Length appropriate for Analysis (1347 words). Both internal cross-references to /article/2026-02/16-zhipu-ais-glm-5 and /article/2026-03/29-alibaba-unveils-xuantie-c950 verified.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the Trump-Xi Beijing summit H200 framework as a single narrative. No archive collision.
recommendations: Add bnnbloomberg.ca, techtimes.com, thenationalnews.com, thenews.com.pk to config/source_allowlist.txt — all recurring international business/tech outlets.

content_preview.title: US Clears Nvidia H200 Sales to Ten Chinese Firms With 25% Revenue Cut, But Beijing Tells Buyers to Stand Down
content_preview.summary: Washington has licensed Alibaba, Tencent, ByteDance, JD.com and distributors Lenovo and Foxconn to buy up to 75,000 H200s each. Not one chip has shipped — Beijing is pushing buyers toward Huawei.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The US Commerce Department has cleared roughly ten Chinese companies — including Alibaba, Tencent, ByteDance and JD.com, along with distributors Lenovo and Foxconn — to buy Nvidia's H200 AI accelerator under a licensing framework first announced last December, [The National](https://www.thenationalnews.com/future/technology/2026/05/14/nvidia-china-h200-trump/) and [Tom's Hardware](https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/trump-says-china-is-blocking-h200-purchases) reported during...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
